events.title: US Navy Establishes Blockade of Iran's Ports
events.desc: The U.S. military has initiated a naval blockade of Iran's ports and coastal areas following the breakdown of peace talks between the U.S. and Iran. Six merchant ships have reportedly turned back, adhering to the blockade orders. This action applies to all Iranian ports on the Gulf and Gulf of Oman.

events.title: Israeli Strikes Kill Multiple People in Gaza Refugee Camp
events.desc: An Israeli airstrike hit the Shati refugee camp in Gaza, resulting in casualties. The exact number of fatalities and injuries is still being assessed, with victims being transported to medical facilities for treatment.

events.title: Israeli Airstrike Kills Child in Gaza Police Car
events.desc: An Israeli airstrike targeted a police vehicle in Gaza City, resulting in the deaths of at least four individuals, including a three-year-old child.

events.title: Iran's Asymmetric Counterair Campaign Continues with Attacks on US Air Base
events.desc: Iranian drones and missiles struck Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, destroying an E-3 Sentry and damaging KC-135 tankers. This is part of a broader counterair campaign waged through asymmetric means in the ongoing conflict.
events.location: Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia

events.title: US Appears to Have Deployed Mines Over Iranian Village
events.desc: Evidence suggests the U.S. deployed the Gator Scatterable Mine system over Kafari, a village near Shiraz, Iran, resulting in casualties. Experts identified the munitions as likely U.S.-made Gator anti-tank mines.

events.title: Two Waves of Bombing Hit IRGC Compound and School in Minab, Iran
events.desc: New videos confirm two waves of missile strikes hit an IRGC compound and an adjacent school in Minab, Iran. The strikes, released by Iran's Ministry of Foreign Affairs, show multiple missiles impacting the complex.

events.title: Centcom Chief Visits Israel and Bahrain Amid Fragile Ceasefire and Blockade
events.desc: The commander of U.S. Central Command visited Israel and Bahrain to garner support for opening the Strait of Hormuz and discuss the ongoing maritime blockade of Iran's ports amid a fragile ceasefire.

events.title: Israel-Lebanon Talks Conclude with Focus on Opposing Hezbollah
events.desc: Direct talks between Israel and Lebanon in Washington concluded with Israeli Ambassador Yechiel Leiter stating both countries are aligned against Iran-backed Hezbollah, aiming to 'liberate Lebanon' from its influence.

events.title: US Democrats Vow to Continue Efforts to Limit Trump's War Powers
events.desc: The U.S. Senate will vote on the latest Democratic attempt to curb President Trump's war powers, with party leaders promising to repeatedly introduce such resolutions as long as the Iran war persists. They criticize Republican colleagues for being sidelined by fear of Trump.

events.title: Lebanon Seeks Peace Amidst Hezbollah's Continued Attacks
events.desc: Despite Lebanon's desire for peace, the government has limited influence over Hezbollah, which continues its attacks as peace talks loom. Israel claims Lebanon is not included in the U.S.-Iran ceasefire, leading to intensified Israeli strikes.

events.title: Israeli Strikes Intensify in Lebanon Amidst Looming Talks
events.desc: Israel has escalated its strikes in Lebanon, killing over 160 people since Friday, despite the U.S.-Iran ceasefire. Hezbollah's attacks continue, creating a volatile situation as peace talks approach.

events.title: Hungarian Government Passwords Exposed Online
events.desc: Approximately 800 Hungarian government email addresses and passwords have been leaked online, compromising the security of 12 out of 13 ministries. The exposed credentials included basic vulnerabilities and potentially sensitive information.

events.title: Iran Imposes Near-Total Internet Blackout During Strikes
events.desc: Following Israeli and American strikes, Iran experienced a 98% collapse in internet traffic, indicating a near-total blackout. This shutdown was imposed by the government on its own citizens, hindering communication.

events.title: Satellite Provider Restricts Imagery of Iran Amidst US Pressure
events.desc: Planet Labs, a satellite imagery provider, has indefinitely restricted its coverage of Iran and parts of the Middle East. This decision follows unspecified pressure, impacting open-source intelligence gathering capabilities in the region.

events.title: Iran's Nuclear Program Sustains Setbacks but Highly Enriched Uranium Stockpile Intact
events.desc: Recent U.S. and Israeli attacks have damaged Iran's nuclear and ballistic capabilities, setting back its nuclear program. However, the stockpile of highly enriched uranium, crucial for negotiations, remains unaffected, according to experts and diplomatic sources.
